content.problemStatement:
You are given the sequence of Nucleotides of one strand of DNA through a string SSS of length NNN. SSS contains the character A,T,C,A, T, C,A,T,C, and GGG only.

Chef knows that:


AAA is complementary to TTT.
TTT is complementary to AAA.
CCC is complementary to GGG.
GGG is complementary to CCC.
Using the string SSS, determine the sequence of the complementary strand of the DNA.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
First line of each test case contains an integer NNN - denoting the length of string SSS.
Second line contains NNN characters denoting the string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output the string containing NNN characters - sequence of nucleotides of the complementary strand.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the DNA strand. The second line contains a string S of length N consisting only of characters 'A', 'T', 'C', and 'G'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the complementary DNA strand of length N.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each nucleotide is replaced by its complement: A↔T, C↔G.
C→G, G→C, A→T, T→A, G→C, C→G resulting in GCTACG.

solution.approach: For each character of the given strand, replace it with its complement: A↔T and C↔G. This can be done in a single pass over the string. Use a simple lookup (switch/case or dictionary) to map each nucleotide to its complement. The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) where N is the length of each string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the output string of each test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        s = next(it)
        comp = {'A':'T','T':'A','C':'G','G':'C'}
        out_lines.append(''.join(comp[ch] for ch in s))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
